Transcrição Completa: Day 30 - Conclusão do Desafio de 30 Dias de Vendas

 

 

[00:00]"Congratulations. Oh my good man. You made it all the way to day 30."

 

[00:04]"I'm proud of you if you've actually hit your 100 per day."

 

[00:08]"If you didn't hit the right numbers out of all this entire sales day challenges that you just took, dude, you're the one who needs to build your skills."

 

[00:16]"You're the one who needs to get better. And that's the exact point of life, guys."

 

[00:21]"You have to be able to get better, build your skills, and be humbled enough to do it."

 

[00:26]"Get your ass back to day one if you didn't hit your numbers."

 

[00:30]"Get your ass back to day one if you're not satisfied with your sales."

 

[00:34]"But more importantly, the only thing holding you back now is your own mindset and the limiting beliefs that you allow yourself to convince yourself are holding you back."

 

[00:44]"You're probably aware now that sometimes you've had thoughts where I don't know if I want to do that."

 

[00:50]"Figured out, I don't actually think that. And that's pretty dumb that I did think that."

 

[00:55]"Now I understand that I just didn't know what I didn't know."

 

[00:59]"And because I now know this, you're telling me there's another thing that I don't know that I don't know."

 

[01:04]"And another thing that I don't know that I don't know that's going to get me more results. Oh my goodness."

 

[01:10]"And every person that I'm going to hire also is in a state where they don't know what they don't know. Oh my god."

 

[01:17]"Everybody's in a state where they don't know anything. And I don't want to hold that against you, but you don't need to hold that against yourself."

 

[01:24]"But you have to be aware of it."

 

[01:26]"You probably tried to convince yourself a few times, maybe even including right now, that whatever it is that's actually true isn't true."

 

[01:34]"That's crazy, but that might be you. And if that is you, get your ass back to day one."

 

[01:40]"Hit your 100 per day. There's no excuses. Build your business."

 

[01:45]"And hey, if you really want to cash out, go ahead and take the 60-day sales challenge."

 

[01:50]"Go ahead and take the 90-day sales challenge."

 

[01:53]"Go ahead and get in a position where you go all the way back to day one and just go again and again and again and again and again."

 

[02:00]"Get your sales people on it. Build your business. You can have a cash cow operation."

 

[02:06]"If you guys need quick delivery once again, like you're like, Jeremy, I just made it rain, dude."

 

[02:11]"Like, how do I deliver on all these deals? I'm going to try to catch up for a second before I take another 30-day sales challenge, man."

 

[02:18]"I got a lot to get ahead on. It's in the search function once again. Outsourcing 101."

 

[02:24]"You can find it really easily. It'll show you in under 20 minutes how to step by step go from wherever you're at to having an entire outsourcing fleet of freelancers executing on your behalf."

 

[02:36]"You can hire virtual assistants to create lead lists for you in the meantime."

 

[02:40]"So, when you're ready for your next 30-day sales challenge, you got a whole vault of people that you're about to slam because you realized that that's over 3,000 people that you just had to communicate with."

 

[02:51]"Where did you get 3,000 emails from? You went to Hunter.io."

 

[02:55]"You used things like the mail verification tool where you had the capacity to validate emails in real time."

 

[03:02]"You've gone to their Facebooks, you've sent them messages, you've gone to their LinkedIn and got emails, you've gone to their straight websites and pulled emails off."

 

[03:11]"You've used who is and you've pulled emails off of there as well in real time."

 

[03:16]"You have the capacity right now to overdeliver on everything that you've just done for the other people that you're about to hire in since you just went through this whole process and you now know how to make it better for whatever niche, industry, and type of person that you're selling."

 

[03:32]"Go build your business. I'm glad you took the 30-day sales challenge."

 

[03:37]"And once again, if you didn't hit the numbers, get your ass back to day one."

 

[03:42]"And if you did hit your numbers, go ahead and comment below. What number did you end up hitting?"

 

[03:47]"What amount of clients did you close? How many people hit you back in a day?"

 

[03:52]"Go ahead and throw some of your stats below. Go ahead and let other people know, like a leaderboard."

 

[03:57]"Throw up your numbers, baby. Who won the game?"

 

 

📊 RESUMO EXECUTIVO

 

NÚMEROS PRINCIPAIS:

Meta de 100 contatos por dia durante 30 dias

Mais de 3.000 pessoas para comunicar ao longo do desafio

Tempo de treinamento de outsourcing: menos de 20 minutos

Desafios adicionais disponíveis: 60 dias e 90 dias

 

ESTRATÉGIAS E SISTEMAS:

Desafio de 30 Dias de Vendas com meta diária de 100 contatos

Sistema de outsourcing através de freelancers e assistentes virtuais

Múltiplas fontes de captura de leads: Hunter.io, Facebook, LinkedIn, websites, WHOIS

Sistema de verificação de emails em tempo real

Processo de construção de listas de leads através de VAs

Estratégia de repetição do desafio para melhoria contínua

 

ESTRUTURA:

Dia 30: Conclusão e avaliação do desafio

Sistema de "voltar ao dia 1" para quem não atingiu metas

Progressão escalonada: 30 → 60 → 90 dias de desafio

Framework de "não saber o que não sabe" para desenvolvimento contínuo

Estrutura de leaderboard/competição através de comentários

Recurso "Outsourcing 101" disponível na função de busca

 

DADOS FINANCEIROS:

Modelo de "cash cow operation" mencionado

Capacidade de "fazer chover" (fechar múltiplos contratos)

Necessidade de estrutura de entrega rápida para escalar

 

FERRAMENTAS MENCIONADAS:

Hunter.io (captura de emails)

Ferramenta de verificação de emails

Facebook Messenger

LinkedIn

WHOIS

Recurso "Outsourcing 101"

 

MINDSET E MENTALIDADE:

Eliminação de crenças limitantes

Consciência sobre "não saber o que não sabe"

Humildade para recomeçar e melhorar

Responsabilidade pessoal pelos resultados

Mentalidade de repetição e persistência

 

DURAÇÃO: ~4 minutos FORMATO: Vídeo motivacional de conclusão/call to action
